title: IBM Cloud Object Storage access keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: IBM Cloud Object Storage HMAC Access Key ID detected. This 32-character hexadecimal credential provides S3-compatible API access to IBM Cloud Object Storage buckets and objects. When paired with a secret access key, it allows authenticated read/write operations on stored data. Exposing this key could allow unauthorized access to storage buckets, data exfiltration, data manipulation, and incur storage and bandwidth charges on your IBM Cloud account.

IBM Cloud Object Storage HMAC Secret Access Key detected. This 56-character credential is the secret component of IBM Cloud Object Storage HMAC authentication, providing S3-compatible API access. When combined with an access key ID, it grants full access to storage operations including reading, writing, and deleting objects. Exposing this secret allows unauthorized parties to access all data in associated buckets, manipulate stored objects, and incur significant storage and bandwidth costs. Revoke this credential immediately in the IBM Cloud console and rotate all affected keys.
